message:
test(fuzz): add CBOR Tag rejection fuzz target across all positions (resolves ym1jq)

canonicalize_value_in_place (lib.rs:484-513) explicitly rejects every
Value::Tag with UnsupportedTag — tags are not part of canonical CBOR
per RFC 8949 §4.2. A regression that allowed tags through anywhere
would let an attacker smuggle non-canonical wire forms past the
canonical-encoding gate.

Existing canonicalize_map_deterministic explicitly skips tags ("we also
avoid Tag... not a canonicalization property to probe here"). NOT
covered as a discrete cross-position MR.

Six properties covering Tag at every structural position:
- Tag at root → UnsupportedTag
- Tag inside Array → UnsupportedTag (recursion catches it)
- Tag as Map value → UnsupportedTag
- Tag as Map key → UnsupportedTag
- Tag-in-Tag → UnsupportedTag (outer rejected first)
- tag-free structural-twin canonicalizes successfully (acceptance
  branch keeps rejection assertions non-vacuous)

Once-gated regression anchors:
- (a) Tag values {0, 1, 12_345, u64::MAX} at every position
  (root, Array, Map value, Map key, nested Array, Tag-in-Tag) MUST
  trip UnsupportedTag
- (b) Structurally-equivalent tag-free Values (Null, Array of Null,
  Map with Null) MUST canonicalize cleanly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -686,5 +686,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_cbor_tag_rejection"
+path = "fuzz_targets/cbor_tag_rejection.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
